message:
fix(demo): use Unicode-aware string length for text truncation

Fixes potential panics and incorrect truncation when titles or text
contain multi-byte Unicode characters:

command_palette.rs:
- Use .chars().count() for title width calculation
- Use .chars().take(n).collect() for safe truncation
- Prevents panic when truncating mid-character

guided_tour.rs:
- Same Unicode-safe approach for tour step titles and tips
- Proper character counting for modal title centering

docs.rs:
- Unicode-safe title truncation in header bar
- Correct width calculation for right-aligned info

Before: `&title[..n]` could panic if n lands inside a multi-byte char
After: `title.chars().take(n).collect()` safely handles any Unicode

This ensures the TUI renders correctly with non-ASCII content like
emoji, CJK characters, or accented letters in documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
